title: Camp Three (Serroco)
content:

{{Quote|Aren't you guys setting up awfully close to the cities?|Zayne Carrick, to Carth Onasi|<ref name="kotor14" />}}
'''Camp Three''' was a [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] [[Republic Military/Legends|military]] camp that was established on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Serroco]] when the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior clans [[Battle of Serroco|threatened]] the world in [[3963 BBY]], during the [[Mandalorian Wars]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] and the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]]. Placed near one of Serroco's [[Stereb]] [[Stereb cities|cities]] by the order of Republic [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Saul Karath]], Camp Three was host to the ''[[Little Bivoli]]'' mess [[Starship/Legends|ship]], which quickly became extremely popular among the servicemen stationed at the settlement.

When the Mandalorians arrived at Serroco, they unleashed a barrage of [[Nuclear weapon/Legends|nuclear bombs]] that annihilated the Republic camps, including Camp Three, out of contempt for what [[Chief/Legends|chief]] [[Mandalore the Ultimate]] saw as an honorless defense. Not everyone stationed at Camp Three was [[Death/Legends|killed]], however—''Little Bivoli'' proprietor [[Marn Hierogryph]] and his [[Chef/Legends|cook]], [[Slyssk]], stole a Republic [[Troop/Legends|troop]] [[Transport/Legends|transport]] and escaped in the nick of [[Time/Legends|time]], saving many [[Republic soldier]]s in the process.

==Description==
{{Quote|Hard enough keeping clean in this mudhole without this stuff happening!|A Republic soldier at Camp Three, after a [[Service droid/Legends|serving droid]] spilled food on his uniform|<ref name="kotor14" />}}
Camp Three was one of the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy's]] [[Republic Military/Legends|military]] camps on the muddy and [[Water/Legends|rainy]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Serroco]] during the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. As per [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Navy/Legends|Navy]] strategy, the camp was located on the edge of one of Serroco's [[Stereb cities]], large settlements of [[Stone/Legends|stone]] towers and [[pyramid]]s constructed by the native [[Stereb]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. The camp comprised a number of [[Starship/Legends|ships]], both Republic and [[Fringer/Legends|fringer]], that served as [[Barracks/Legends|barracks]], [[Restaurant/Legends|restaurants]], and other establishments for [[Republic soldier]]s. These ships included numerous [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruisers]],<ref name="kotor14" /> [[Troop/Legends|troop]] [[Transport/Legends|transports]] that housed naval personnel,<ref name="kotor47">[[Knights of the Old Republic 47|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 47]]</ref> and the ''[[Little Bivoli]]'', a fringer mess ship that became extremely popular amongst the men of Camp Three.<ref name="kotor14" />

Featuring the talented [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] [[Chef/Legends|cook]] [[Slyssk]], the ''Little Bivoli'' was actually run by two of the Republic's most wanted fugitives—[[Zayne Carrick]], a former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] wanted for [[Gender/Legends|his]] involvement in the [[Padawan Massacre]] of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]], and the [[Snivvian/Legends|Snivvian]] [[Marn Hierogryph]], his "accomplice." Situated as the compound was on the "mudhole" Serroco, Republic personnel had trouble staying dry at Camp Three. Other than the [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]], Serroco's native Stereb also wandered the camp, eating scraps.<ref name="kotor14" /> [[Atmosphere/Legends|Air]] traffic to and from Camp Three was directed by a manned control tower, and surrounding [[Speaker/Legends|loudspeakers]] alerted Republic servicemen to enemy forces in the [[Serroco system/Legends|system]]. In case of an attack, Camp Three, like the other camps on Serroco, was equipped with a [[Missile/Legends|missile]] defense system, but it was not kept online when no assault was expected.<ref name="kotor15" />

==History==
{{Quote|We've been recognized as ''the place to be in Camp Three,'' that's what!|Marn Hierogryph, proprietor of the ''Little Bivoli'', to Zayne Carrick|<ref name="kotor14" />}}
[[File:BivoliCampThree.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The ''Little Bivoli'', situated at Camp Three]]
In [[3963 BBY]], the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior clans, in the midst of their [[Mandalorian Wars|campaign]] against the Galactic Republic, threatened the<ref name="kotor14" /> [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> world of Serroco, a full member of the Republic. Anticipating a Mandalorian attack, Republic Navy [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Saul Karath]] organized a [[Battle Group Serroco|battle group]] and set off to defend Serroco. Karath's strategy involved placing his camps close to the Stereb [[City/Legends|cities]], believing that the Mandalorians would avoid attacking them directly out of fear of wrecking valuable loot.<ref name="kotor14" />

According to this edict, Camp Three was built on the edge of one of the Stereb cities, with spacelift jockeys like hotshot [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Carth Onasi]] landing many of the ships that comprised the settlement. Karath personally gave Camp Three a walking tour when the place was settled. The ''Little Bivoli'' eatery rapidly became one of Camp Three's go-to spots, earning proprietor Marn Hierogryph a great amount of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|money]]—his partner, wanted Republic fugitive Zayne Carrick, still chafed, wanting the chance to clear his name. With the Mandalorians approaching, Carrick, who already had been trying to convince Hierogryph to raise shop, had a [[Force vision/Legends|Force vision]] of the Mandalorians destroying Serroco.<ref name="kotor14" />

Perturbed, Carrick decided that he needed to warn Karath,<ref name="kotor14" /> and he stowed away on Onasi's [[Deadweight|ship]] from Camp Three to Karath's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Courageous (Inexpugnable-class)|''Courageous'']].<ref name="kotor15" /> Carrick made it to the ship's [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] but was recognized and detained—soon afterward, Mandalorian forces arrived in the [[Star system/Legends|system]], setting off the proximity alarms at Camp Three. The Mandalorians started off the [[Battle of Serroco|proceedings]] by firing a barrage of [[Nuclear weapon/Legends|nuclear missiles]], and Carrick could only watch as his vision came true. Initially appearing to be headed for Karath's ships, the missiles angled around the Republic [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], aimed at the camps all along, [[Mandalore the Ultimate]]'s way of punishing the Republic for what he saw as an honorless defense. The missile defense system at Camp Three was not operational at the [[Time/Legends|time]], and the camp was obliterated.<ref name="kotor15" />

Despite the destruction of the camp, some were able to escape. Hierogryph and Slyssk from the ''Little Bivoli'' were able to charge aboard and steal a troop transport and get off Serroco in the nick of time, inadvertently saving half a [[Sleep/Legends|sleeping]] [[Battalion/Legends|battalion]] in the process. For this act, they were honored as Republic heroes.<ref name="kotor47" />

==Behind the scenes==
Camp Three first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Days of Fear]]'', a story arc from the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[John Jackson Miller]] and released in [[2007]].<ref name="kotor14" /><ref name="kotor15" /> Camp Three would also appear in a flashback in a later issue from the series, [[Knights of the Old Republic 47|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 47]].<ref name="kotor47" /> During its appearances, Camp Three was illustrated by [[Brian Ching]]<ref name="kotor14" /><ref name="kotor47" /> and [[Dustin Weaver]].<ref name="kotor15" />
